title: hieracium murorum subsp. suppinatum
benefits: Hieracium murorum subsp. suppinatum has many benefits, such as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with arthritis, gout, and other inflammatory conditions. The plant's antioxidant properties may also help protect against cell damage, potentially reducing the risk of certain cancers and neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's and Parkinson's. Additionally, Hieracium murorum subsp. suppinatum may exhibit antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it a useful natural remedy for wound healing, skin infections, and fungal infections of the nail and scalp. By harnessing the medicinal properties of this plant, individuals may experience improved overall health, increased energy levels, and enhanced quality of life.
parts: Hieracium murorum subsp. suppinatum has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, flowers, and roots. The leaves of Hieracium murorum subsp. suppinatum contain sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flowers of this plant contain flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, which are reported to have antimicrobial and cytotoxic effects. Additionally, the roots of Hieracium murorum subsp. suppinatum contain sesquiterpene lactones and phenolic acids, which may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
side_effects: Hieracium murorum subsp. suppinatum can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and increased heart rate. Ingesting large quantities of the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Skin contact with the plant may lead to skin irritation, redness, and itching. Prolonged exposure to its toxic compounds can also cause liver and kidney damage in severe cases.
